elections.note: Certified final 40,325 ballots cast (Total Voters) from CA SoS 'Voter Participation Statistics by County', complete 2012 SOV (Madera: 96 precincts, 86,894 eligible, 53,779 registered, 15,658 precinct + 24,667 VBM = 40,325). Election-night plateau NOT recoverable: this predates Madera's Clarity ENR (Madera's first Clarity election is the June 2018 primary, id 75694) and its WordPress/votemadera.com results system. Wayback's earliest votemadera.com capture is the homepage 02/02/2011, then nothing until Oct 2014; the /results/ folder is first archived as Election28 = Nov 2014. No 2012 election-night results page, semi-final canvass PDF, or news-quoted county ballot total found. No snapshot used. RECOVERY ATTEMPT (2026-07-09): per the Task 1 NewsBank coverage probe, Madera Tribune has ZERO indexed text for all of calendar 2012 (confirmed archive gap, not a query artifact; not re-probed), so Fresno Bee is the only paper in play. Searched Fresno Bee, Newspaper/Text, Nov 7-9 2012, queries 'Madera ballots', 'Madera County votes counted', 'Madera turnout', 'Madera precincts reporting', 'Madera election results', 'Madera County' (phrase), 'Madera registrar', 'Madera votes', 'Madera Chowchilla ballots', plus a title-field 'Madera' sweep (0 hits): no article gives a Madera countywide ballots-cast total or a semi-final table for election night. One candidate examined and REJECTED: 'Valley ballot-counting continues' (Fresno Bee, Nov 8 2012 pA3, docref news/142721EBD5B41378) quotes Madera County Clerk Rebecca Martinez: 'In Madera County, voter turnout is 61%, but could hit 70% once remaining ballots are counted... Four years ago, the turnout was 79.7%.' This is (a) a REGISTERED-VOTER-denominator turnout percent, not ballots/certified-final, (b) explicitly self-described as NOT final by the clerk herself ('could hit 70%'), and (c) dated Nov 8 (Thursday, two days post-election-night), a mid-canvass status, not the last report posted on election night. Per RUNBOOK 5.2 and the brief's explicit caution against the Santa Clara 2016 near-final-snapshot mistake, this is folded here as an examined dead end rather than landed as a floor/ceiling. Row remains null. DEAD-END NOTE EXTENSION (2026-07-09, Task 9): checked whether Madera's elections CMS (votemadera.com, WordPress) or its parent domain (maderacounty.com) runs CivicPlus DocumentCenter -- neither is a CivicPlus site at all (votemadera.com is WordPress; the 2012 election predates Madera's Clarity ENR adoption, see the note above), so no ID-walk was possible. The brief's suggested registrar-social accounts (@sacvote, @sdvote, @SBCountyROV, @RivCoVote, @FresnoCoClerk) are all for OTHER counties; no Madera-specific registrar Twitter/Facebook handle was named or found via Wayback CDX guessing. No new route found; row remains as documented above, NewsBank-exhausted per the prior dated correction.

Certified final 27,370 ballots cast (Total Voters) from CA SoS 'Voter Participation Statistics by County', 2014 complete SOV (Madera: 100 precincts, 85,976 eligible, 52,494 registered, 8,970 precinct + 18,400 VBM = 27,370). Election-night plateau NOT recoverable as a clean number. The only archived data state of votemadera.com's result28.htm / resultsc28.htm (captures 20141108134805 and 20141118031430) is the report 'Election Results as of 11/07/2014 at 02:14:32 PM' = Total Ballots Cast 26,449 (precinct 8,106 + absentee 18,343), 100 of 100 precincts -- this is the FIRST CANVASS UPDATE (Friday 11/7 afternoon, ~96.6% of certified), NOT the election-night semifinal. Madera's own 'Updates to Election Night Results' page (capture 20141111035904) confirms 4,385 late VBM 'arrived too late to be processed and included in the Semifinal Results from Election Night' and were counted starting 'Friday 11/7 at 1:00pm', so the true election-night plateau (which excluded those 4,385) was overwritten in place and is unarchived. No 2014 Clarity ENR exists (Madera's first Clarity is June 2018), so the live-CDN version recovery used for 2018/2022 is not available here. Recording 26,449 would overstate the election-night share, so left null. RECOVERY (2026-07-09): both papers agree the sheriff and DA races reached 100 of 100 precincts reporting Tuesday night (Fresno Bee 'Varney, Linn roll to wins in Madera', Nov 5 2014 pA6: Varney 57.8%/Salvador 41.9%, Linn 58.5%/Keitz 41.2%; Madera Tribune 'Police chief to be new sheriff' and 'A Linn win for district attorney', Nov 5 2014 pA1, same percentages, 'with all precincts reporting Tuesday evening'), but neither paper prints a raw countywide ballot total for election night itself. The Madera Tribune's Friday edition, 'More than 5,300 votes left to count' (Nov 7 2014 pA1, docref news/15175914B6DDC6C0), quotes Madera County Clerk-Recorder Rebecca Martinez: 4,385 vote-by-mail ballots 'were received by her office and 100 polling places after noon on Tuesday's Election Day' (i.e., too late for the night's tally) plus 973 provisional ballots (requiring eligibility verification over following weeks) equal 'the 5,358 ballots left to be counted' (4,385+973=5,358 exactly). Certified final 27,370 minus the 5,358 ballots explicitly documented as NOT part of the night's tally = 22,012 ballots counted as of election night; this matches the same article's own framing, 'this week's election saw more than 22,000 votes' (22,012>22,000). This is treated as a FLOOR (RUNBOOK 5.2): the article frames the late-VBM/provisional split as the only outstanding categories as of Friday, but a small amount of additional routine VBM processing between Tuesday night and the Friday cutoff cannot be fully ruled out. pct = 22,012/27,370 = 80.42%. Self-consistent with the existing 'not recoverable' finding above: the Friday 2:14pm capture (26,449) is 4,437 higher than 22,012, close to the 4,385 late-VBM batch the article says 'will likely be counted by 5 tonight' (Friday), i.e. that capture already reflects most of the late batch being added. Calibration: 80.42% sits alongside 2016's 80.0% (both pre-e-pollbook, traditional-polling-place era, small precinct-based county that counts fast), slightly above the brief's a-priori 65-80% midterm estimate but explained by the adjacent same-era presidential year, not a first-tranche or canvass-contaminated value (100/100 precincts, only the well-quantified late-VBM/provisional gap remains, which is exactly what the plateau definition expects). PLAUSIBLE not CONFIRMED: no independent Clarity/report bracket proves this was the LAST report of the night (Madera's Clarity ENR did not exist until 2018), only the clerk's own quantified breakdown of what remained uncounted. NewsBank access: SFPL ezproxy, Fresno Bee and Madera Tribune, Newspaper/Text, Nov 5-14 2014 windows, queries 'Madera ballots'/'Madera County votes counted'/'Madera turnout'/'Madera County'/'Madera precincts reporting' plus follow-up sweeps ('Martinez ballots', 'vote', 'election', cadence sweep) that located the Nov 7 and Nov 11 Tribune follow-ups. CORRECTION (2026-07-09): relabeled CEILING and flagged comparable=false: the 5,358-remaining snapshot is from Friday Nov 7, so any interim processing beyond the two named categories can only make the true election-night count lower than 22,012; the prior FLOOR label had the risk direction backwards. The article's 'this week's election saw more than 22,000 votes' framing is retained as reported language but is not independent corroboration of 22,012 (likely the same certified-minus-remaining subtraction the reporter would have done); its exact wording was not re-verified against the source in this pass.

Numerator 35,364 = the election-night Semi-Final Official Canvass total from votemadera.com's static results page resultsc33.htm/result33.htm, header 'Election Results as of 11/08/2016 at 11:31:17 PM', 102 of 102 precincts completed: Precinct Ballots Cast 12,803 + Absentee Ballots Cast 22,561 = Total Ballots Cast 35,364 (registered 58,128). This is the late-election-night plateau (11:31 PM, all precinct results in). It HELD STEADY past election night: the same '11/08/2016 11:31:17 PM' / 35,364 state appears in both the Nov 12 capture (resultsc33.htm, snapshot 20161112115134) and the Dec 30 capture (result33.htm, 20161230001056), confirming it was the frozen election-night semifinal before the multi-day canvass. Certified final 44,186 (CA SoS 'Voter Participation Statistics by County'; Madera 102 precincts, 87,254 eligible, 58,086 registered, 15,211 precinct + 28,975 VBM). pct = 35,364 / 44,186 = 80.03% (rounded 80.0%). Snapshot 20161112115134 used.

Numerator 28,159 = total Ballots Cast (Contests[0].BC) from the Madera County Clarity ENR election-night data version 220349 (results.enr.clarityelections.com/CA/Madera/92458/220349/json/sum.json), recovered from the LIVE Clarity CDN (immutable version-pinned data) and freshly archived to Wayback (snapshot 20260627203408, verified BC=28,159, 62 of 69 precincts). The version's electionsettings.json gives websiteupdatedat = '11/6/2018 11:53:23 PM PST' -- the LAST election-night report. It is the election-night plateau and it HELD STEADY: the next two published versions 220492 ('11/7/2018 9:34:48 AM PST') and 220715 ('11/8/2018 11:00:04 AM PST') still show BC=28,159; only on 11/9 (version 221196, '11/9/2018 4:17:42 PM PST') does the canvass resume at BC=32,380. Election-night progression on 11/6: v219858 (8:59 PM, 24,508) -> v220100 (9:53 PM, 24,711) -> v220277 (11:06 PM, 26,408) -> v220324 (11:32 PM, 27,262) -> v220349 (11:53 PM, 28,159). Certified final 38,968 (CA SoS 'Voter Participation Statistics by County'; Madera 89,818 eligible, 57,418 registered) -- also equals BC of the Clarity certified version 222713 ('11/21/2018 12:58:53 PM PST', BC=38,968). pct = 28,159 / 38,968 = 72.26%. First general under e-pollbooks / VCA vote centers (adopted 2018). SUPERSEDES the prior 'not recoverable' finding: the Wayback ENR captures of id 92458 lacked the data JSON (only a Nov 29 post-cert index), but the live Clarity CDN still serves every published version and its electionsettings 'versions' array enumerates them. Snapshot 20260627203408 used.

Numerator 21,951 = total Ballots Cast (Contests[0].BC) from the Madera County Clarity ENR election-night data version 311779 (results.enr.clarityelections.com/CA/Madera/116174/311779/json/sum.json), recovered from the LIVE Clarity CDN (immutable version-pinned data) and freshly archived to Wayback (snapshot 20260627203522, verified BC=21,951, 50 of 59 precincts). The version's electionsettings.json gives websiteupdatedat = '11/8/2022 10:44:29 PM PST' -- the LAST election-night report. It is the election-night plateau: the very next published version 312400 is dated '11/10/2022 4:11:23 PM PST' (BC jumps to 25,243), i.e. the county stopped publishing after 11/8 ~10:44 PM and resumed the canvass on 11/10, so 21,951 is the frozen end-of-election-night number. Election-night progression on 11/8: v311053 (8:09 PM, 18,832) -> v311314 (8:47 PM, 19,335) -> v311666 (10:04 PM, 20,099) -> v311726 (10:23 PM, 21,109) -> v311779 (10:44 PM, 21,951). Certified final 37,345 (CA SoS 'Voter Participation Statistics by County'; Madera 59 precincts, 93,789 eligible, 72,865 registered, 3,333 precinct + 34,012 VBM) -- also equals BC of the Clarity certified version 314277 ('11/23/2022 9:30:52 AM PST', BC=37,345). pct = 21,951 / 37,345 = 58.78%. SUPERSEDES the prior 'not recoverable' finding: the Wayback capture of the live ENR page (id 116174) returned HTTP 403 (CloudFront), but the live Clarity CDN still serves every published data version and its electionsettings 'versions' array (18 entries, 305400..314277) enumerates them. Snapshot 20260627203522 used.

Numerator 37,106 = total Ballots Cast (field BC) from the Madera County Clarity ENR election-night data version 353191 (results.enr.clarityelections.com/CA/Madera/122832/353191/json/sum.json), recovered via Wayback (snapshot 20241108001837). The version's electionsettings.json gives websiteupdatedat = '11/5/2024 11:51:26 PM PST' -- the late/end-of-election-night report, 63 of 78 precincts reporting, registered 78,317. It is the election-night plateau: the next archived version 353996 is a Nov 9 update where BC jumps to 44,900 (64/78), so 37,106 held from late election night through the next days before the canvass resumed. Certified final 55,329 (CA SoS 'Voter Participation Statistics by County'; Madera 78 precincts, 95,290 eligible, 78,204 registered, 8,560 precinct + 46,769 VBM). pct = 37,106 / 55,329 = 67.06% (rounded 67.1%). Snapshot 20241108001837 used. NOTE on trend: 2016 (pre-epollbook, traditional polling places) plateaued at 80.0% vs 2024 (post-epollbook, universal-VBM vote centers) at 67.1% -- the drop tracks the statewide shift to universal vote-by-mail (more late-arriving ballots), not e-pollbook check-in speed per se. Like-to-like midterms: 2018 (first VCA year) 72.26% -> 2022 58.78%, same downward direction.
